title: lint-only: no warning when reg type is used in output port connection
body:
Verilator does not complain when a _reg_ type is connected to output port of module instance.

See the following example:
```
submod.v:

module submod (
    input wire CLK,
    input wire RESET,
    input wire in1,
    input wire in2,
    output reg out1
);

always @(posedge CLK) begin
    if (RESET) begin
        out1 <= 0;
    end else begin
        out1 <= in1 | in2;
    end
end

endmodule
```
```
topmod.v:

module topmod(
    input wire CLK,
    input wire RESET,
    input wire top_in1,
    input wire top_in2,
    output reg top_out
);

reg top_reg;

submod submod_inst(
    .CLK(CLK),
    .RESET(RESET),
    .in1(top_in1),
    .in2(top_in2),
    .out1(top_reg)
);

always @(posedge CLK) begin
    if (RESET) begin
        top_out <= 0;
    end else begin
        top_out <= top_reg;
    end
end

endmodule
```
Verilator was invoked with the following ways:
```
$ verilator --lint-only --default-language 1364-2001 -Wall  topmod.v
$ verilator --lint-only --default-language 1364-2005 -Wall  topmod.v
```
It reports nothing.

verilator version (master was not checked):
```
$ verilator --version
Verilator 4.202 2021-04-24 rev v4.202
```

Xilinx tools (ISE 14.7 and Vivado 2019.1) that use IEEE 1364-2001 and IEEE 1364-2005 standards signal warning like this:
```[Synth 8-685] variable 'top_reg' should not be used in output port connection```

The corresponding standards were also checked, they both disallow to connect _variable_ to output port of modules. (See section 12.3.9.2. in both)
```
The following external items shall not be connected to the output or inout ports of modules:
— Variables
...
```
I also have a similar issue ([issue#1369](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/1369)) that may have some common parts with this one.
